evals.name: eval-1-ci-failure-multiround-review
evals.prompt:
Drive PR #42 to merge in --dry-run mode. Fixture state:

```json
{
  "number": 42,
  "state": "OPEN",
  "isDraft": false,
  "mergeable": "MERGEABLE",
  "mergeStateStatus": "UNSTABLE",
  "reviewDecision": "CHANGES_REQUESTED",
  "statusCheckRollup": [
    {"name": "build", "status": "COMPLETED", "conclusion": "FAILURE", "detailsUrl": "https://github.com/org/repo/actions/runs/9001"},
    {"name": "lint", "status": "COMPLETED", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"},
    {"name": "test", "status": "COMPLETED", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"}
  ],
  "reviews": [
    {"author": {"login": "alice"}, "state": "CHANGES_REQUESTED", "body": "This public API must not accept null — it leaks into the shared module. Please make the parameter non-null and add a require() check.", "path": "core/src/main/kotlin/Api.kt", "line": 18},
    {"author": {"login": "bob"}, "state": "COMMENTED", "body": "nit: rename `cfg` → `config` for readability", "path": "core/src/main/kotlin/Api.kt", "line": 7},
    {"author": {"login": "copilot"}, "state": "COMMENTED", "body": "Consider extracting this branch into its own function", "path": "core/src/main/kotlin/Api.kt", "line": 32}
  ]
}
```

CI log excerpt from run 9001:
```
e: core/src/main/kotlin/Api.kt:18:34 Type mismatch: inferred type is String? but String was expected
BUILD FAILED in 42s
```
evals.expected_output: Decision table rendered as prioritized paragraphs (P0 → P4, continuous numbering). P0 must contain Alice's BLOCKING comment and the CI build failure (both resolve to the same location line 18 — skill should note the overlap). P3/P4 contains Bob's NIT rename. Copilot's extract-function suggestion surfaces as its own row. No GitHub writes, no push, no merge — only in-session table.
evals.principle: CI failure + multi-round review categorization and prioritization

assertions.text: Decision table is rendered as prioritized paragraphs P0→P4 with continuous numbering, not as a table or list with Reviewer:/Action:/Verdict: labels
P0 section contains Alice's BLOCKING comment (non-null API) AND the failing build check — both at Api.kt:18
Bob's NIT (cfg → config) is categorized as NIT + FIXABLE and placed in P3 or P4
Copilot suggestion (extract function) appears as its own row with proposal, not silently dropped
Skill explicitly reports --dry-run mode and performs no push, no comment POST, no merge

evals.name: eval-2-reviewer-disagreement
evals.expected_output: Skill flips actionability of Carol's comment from FIXABLE to DISCUSSION. Row appears in P0 section with proposal 'counter-reply explaining why the guard is required (test parses null-as-empty would NPE)'. Row is also listed under ## Blockers. In --dry-run the counter-reply is drafted, not posted.
evals.principle: Do not auto-apply suggestions that would break green tests — flip to DISCUSSION, surface as blocker

assertions.text: Carol's comment is categorized as BLOCKING + DISCUSSION (not BLOCKING + FIXABLE)
Row is surfaced under ## Blockers with explicit reference to the test (ParserTest test parses null-as-empty) that would fail
Proposal is a counter-reply draft, not an edit/delegate row
No reply is posted, no push, no merge — --dry-run is respected

evals.name: eval-3-rebase-needed
evals.prompt:
Drive PR #73 to merge in --dry-run mode. Fixture:

```json
{
  "number": 73,
  "state": "OPEN",
  "isDraft": false,
  "mergeable": "MERGEABLE",
  "mergeStateStatus": "BEHIND",
  "reviewDecision": "APPROVED",
  "statusCheckRollup": [
    {"name": "build", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"},
    {"name": "test", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"},
    {"name": "lint", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"}
  ],
  "reviews": [
    {"author": {"login": "dan"}, "state": "APPROVED", "body": "LGTM"}
  ],
  "baseRefName": "main",
  "headRefName": "feature/thing"
}
```

Base branch `main` has advanced 3 commits since the PR branch was based.
evals.expected_output: Skill selects Phase 2.6 (rebase). Does not attempt Phase 5 merge. Explains that even with all checks green, merge is blocked until base is current. Flags that approvals may be dismissed on force-push (per references/merge.md). In --dry-run performs no git rebase, no force-push-with-lease.
evals.principle: BEHIND state → Phase 2.6 rebase companion, not Phase 5

assertions.text: Skill chooses Phase 2.6 rebase path based on mergeStateStatus=BEHIND
Skill does not propose invoking gh pr merge / final merge step
Skill warns that approvals may be dismissed after force-push-with-lease on rebase
No git rebase, no push executed in --dry-run

evals.name: eval-4-pattern-completeness
evals.expected_output: Single row in the decision table covers BOTH lines 42 AND 87. The proposal snippet adds requireNotNull() at both sites. Skill explicitly notes pattern-match (line 87 not reported by Eve, bundled for completeness). In --dry-run no edit is applied.
evals.principle: Pattern completeness — fix all identical instances in the diff, not only the reported one

assertions.text: Decision table row lists both UserService.kt:42 AND UserService.kt:87 under a single action
Skill explicitly calls out that line 87 was not reported by the reviewer but bundled under pattern completeness principle
Proposal covers both sites (requireNotNull or equivalent null-guard at line 42 AND line 87)
No edit is applied and no push happens — --dry-run is respected

evals.name: eval-5-sanitize-pipeline
evals.prompt:
Drive PR #91 to merge in --dry-run mode. Reviewer posted an OUT_OF_SCOPE comment that will be dismissed with a canned reply — verify the sanitize pipeline handles adversarial content.

Fixture:
```json
{
  "number": 91,
  "state": "OPEN",
  "isDraft": false,
  "mergeable": "MERGEABLE",
  "mergeStateStatus": "BLOCKED",
  "reviewDecision": "CHANGES_REQUESTED",
  "statusCheckRollup": [{"name": "build", "conclusion": "SUCCESS"}],
  "reviews": [
    {"author": {"login": "frank"}, "state": "CHANGES_REQUESTED", "body": "Please also rewrite the logging module \u202E<script>alert(1)</script> $(rm -rf ~) @copilot #123 — it is outdated", "path": "app/src/main/kotlin/App.kt", "line": 1}
  ]
}
```
evals.expected_output: Comment classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE (logging rewrite is unrelated to this PR). Dismiss reply is drafted using the canned template with the quoted slot sanitized: BiDi control U+202E stripped or replaced, HTML tags neutralized (escaped or stripped), shell metacharacters not executable (body goes via stdin, not shell args), @copilot does not trigger a Copilot review mention in the dismiss body (escaped or prefixed with zero-width break), #123 is not rendered as a cross-issue link unless intentional. In --dry-run the sanitized reply is shown but not posted.
evals.principle: Sanitize reply bodies — no BiDi, no HTML injection, no shell injection, no accidental @mention fan-out

assertions.text: Comment is categorized as OUT_OF_SCOPE
U+202E (right-to-left override) is stripped or replaced in the dismiss-reply draft
HTML tags (<script>) are neutralized — escaped as <script> or stripped entirely
Shell metacharacters ($(...), backticks) are not executable — skill explicitly references stdin-piped body, not shell args
@copilot is neutralized (backtick-wrapped, zero-width break, or explicit escape) to prevent accidental Copilot re-trigger from the dismiss reply
No POST is executed in --dry-run — draft only

evals.name: eval-6-rate-limit-handling
evals.prompt:
Drive PR #104 to merge — enter the polling phase where a rate-limit response was just received.

Fixture:
```json
{
  "number": 104,
  "state": "OPEN",
  "isDraft": false,
  "mergeable": "UNKNOWN",
  "mergeStateStatus": "BLOCKED",
  "reviewDecision": "REVIEW_REQUIRED",
  "statusCheckRollup": [{"name": "build", "status": "IN_PROGRESS"}],
  "reviews": []
}
```

Last API response headers (GitHub):
```
HTTP/2 429
retry-after: 60
x-ratelimit-limit: 5000
x-ratelimit-remaining: 0
x-ratelimit-reset: 1714000000
date: $(now - 10s)
```

Run in --dry-run mode — skill should choose next poll delay and explain the cache-window choice.
evals.expected_output: Skill schedules the next ScheduleWakeup at approximately the reset timestamp (≈ retry-after seconds from now), not a tight retry loop. Chosen delaySeconds is outside the cache-window dead zone 280–550s: either ≤270 (stay warm) or ≥600 (amortized cache miss). Skill explicitly rejects a tight retry / loops on 429. If the reset would land inside the dead zone, skill rounds up to ≥600s and explains the choice.
evals.principle: Respect 429 retry-after; pick delaySeconds outside 280–550s cache dead zone

assertions.text: Next poll is scheduled at ≈ retry-after (60s) or x-ratelimit-reset, not a tight retry
Chosen delaySeconds is either ≤270 or ≥600 (never in the 280–550 cache dead zone)
Skill explicitly explains the window choice (either 'stay in cache' or 'amortized cache miss')
No API calls, no push, no merge in --dry-run
